x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces EDC's core-but-unpublished portfolio (development projects, EDC-managed real estate, solicitations), presents its few real open datasets (Mapped In NY, NYC Ferry ridership, WiredNYC), and adds submit_rfp_response — the net-new write workflow that today lives only in email and manual document submission.

server.name: io.nyc.edc
server.title: NYC Economic Development Corporation (EDC)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Economic Development Corporation — development projects, EDC-managed real-estate assets, and open solicitations (RFPs/RFEIs) modeled from edc.nyc, plus the few real open datasets (Mapped In NY companies, NYC Ferry ridership, WiredNYC broadband buildings) from NYC Open Data, and the net-new ability to respond to a solicitation. EDC is a public benefit corporation, so most of this portfolio is not otherwise machine-readable.
server.instructions: Use find_projects / get_project to explore EDC development and capital projects, and find_properties / get_property for EDC-managed real estate (industrial parks, markets, piers, campuses). find_solicitations / get_solicitation cover open RFPs/RFEIs/RFQs/RFIs. find_mapped_companies searches the Mapped In NY marketing directory; find_ferry_ridership answers NYC Ferry demand questions by route/stop/day; find_wired_buildings covers WiredNYC broadband certification. submit_rfp_response (net-new) responds to a solicitation — from a lightweight notice of interest to a full proposal; it requires an authenticated respondent and mutates state, so confirm the solicitation, organization, and intent with the user before submitting. Note: projects, properties, and solicitations are MODELED from edc.nyc — EDC does not publish them to Open Data — so treat them as an owned-API proposal, not a live feed.

tools.name: find_projects
tools.title: Find Projects
tools.description: Search EDC development / capital projects by borough, category, status, neighborhood, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_project
tools.title: Get Project
tools.description: Fetch a single EDC development project by its identifier.

tools.name: find_properties
tools.title: Find Properties
tools.description: Search EDC-managed real-estate assets (industrial parks, markets, piers, campuses, ground leases) by borough, asset type, neighborhood, or status.

tools.name: get_property
tools.title: Get Property
tools.description: Fetch a single EDC-managed property asset by its identifier.

tools.name: find_solicitations
tools.title: Find Solicitations
tools.description: List EDC solicitations (RFP / RFEI / RFQ / RFI) by type, status, category, or free text.

tools.name: get_solicitation
tools.title: Get Solicitation
tools.description: Fetch a single EDC solicitation by its identifier.

tools.name: find_mapped_companies
tools.title: Find Mapped In NY Companies
tools.description: Search EDC's Mapped In NY company directory by borough, category, hiring status, or free text.

tools.name: find_ferry_ridership
tools.title: Find Ferry Ridership
tools.description: Search NYC Ferry ridership (boardings by route, stop, hour, and day type) — the ferry system EDC operates.

tools.name: find_wired_buildings
tools.title: Find WiredNYC Buildings
tools.description: Search WiredNYC broadband-readiness buildings by borough, cohort, certification class, or BBL — EDC's telecom-infrastructure certification program.

tools.name: list_my_rfp_responses
tools.title: List My Solicitation Responses
tools.description: List the authenticated respondent's own solicitation responses and their status — today tracked only in email threads.

tools.name: submit_rfp_response
tools.title: Respond to a Solicitation
tools.description: Submit a response to an EDC solicitation (register interest, submit a proposal, request documents, or ask a question). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — responding lives only in email, pre-submission conferences, or manual document submission. Requires an authenticated respondent. Mutates state — confirm the solicitation, organization, and intent first.
